pulled_artifacts.provider: Copernicus DEM GLO-30 Public on AWS Open Data
caveats: This is a one-degree DSM tile covering the southern portion of the draft Pacuare bounds, not a clipped corridor DEM.
The tile still needs clipping, reprojection, void/artifact review, river-centerline conditioning, and Unreal heightfield import.

pulled_artifacts.provider: Copernicus DEM GLO-30 Public on AWS Open Data
caveats: This is a one-degree DSM tile covering the northern edge of the draft Pacuare bounds, not a clipped corridor DEM.
The tile still needs clipping, reprojection, void/artifact review, river-centerline conditioning, and Unreal heightfield import.

summary.visual_review: best of the sampled dates; partial cloud remains but enough rainforest texture is visible for a coarse Unreal source drape

caveats: MODIS true-color resolution is too coarse for final photoreal corridor art.
The selected image still has partial cloud cover and must be replaced or supplemented with higher-resolution Sentinel/Landsat/first-party imagery before production approval.

summary.visual_review: same selected NASA GIBS date as the retained 512px sample; larger request preserves the active Pacuare preview path at the same nominal source as South Fork and Colorado

caveats: MODIS true-color resolution is still too coarse for final photoreal corridor art even when requested at 1024px.
The selected image still has partial cloud cover and must be replaced or supplemented with higher-resolution Sentinel/Landsat/first-party imagery before production approval.

pulled_artifacts.provider: RaftSim source-preview tool from NASA GIBS and Copernicus DEM GLO-30 inputs
caveats: This is a deterministic source-derived preview composite, not production photoreal imagery.
Cloud-filled areas are DEM-derived rainforest relief placeholders and must be replaced by higher-resolution cloud-screened imagery before production approval.

caveats: This is a deterministic source-derived preview composite, not production photoreal imagery.

pulled_artifacts.provider: RaftSim source-preview tool from Copernicus DEM GLO-30 inputs
caveats: Preview relief is sampled into procedural geometry only; it is not a clipped, reprojected, hydrologically conditioned Unreal heightfield.
Full terrain import still requires proper geospatial conditioning and source-rights review.

pulled_artifacts.provider: RaftSim source-preview tool from Copernicus DEM GLO-30 inputs
caveats: Preview relief is sampled into procedural geometry only; it is not a clipped, reprojected, hydrologically conditioned Unreal heightfield.

pulled_artifacts.provider: RaftSim geospatial preview utility from Copernicus DEM GLO-30 inputs
caveats: This is a normalized import candidate for tool testing, not production terrain.

The source DEM is not yet clipped to a surveyed corridor, reprojected to the selected Costa Rica working CRS, void/artifact reviewed, hydrologically conditioned, river-channel burned, or guide-reviewed.
Unselected samples are retained only as source-selection evidence.
Production imagery still needs scene metadata, acquisition review, and higher-resolution cloud screening.
OpenTopography remains a valid future terrain path, but production pulls require a registered API key and terms review.

production_blockers: Copernicus DEM tiles are downloaded but not clipped, reprojected, hydrologically conditioned, or imported as Unreal heightfields.
NASA GIBS imagery is coarse and partly cloudy; production Pacuare art still needs higher-resolution cloud-screened Sentinel/Landsat/first-party imagery or licensed aerial references.
Authoritative Costa Rica hydrography, rainfall, discharge, access, and protected-area layers are still under source-rights review.
No Pacuare field/social reference media has explicit game-use rights attached yet.
Rain-fed flow bands remain relative until gauge/rainfall sources and guide feedback are reviewed.
